$(li)$(bold)$(l:cooking/decay#oven_baked)Oven Baking$()$(li)$(bold)$(l:cooking/decay#cellar)Cellars$()$(li2)$(l:cooking/decay#shelf)Cellar Storage$()$(li)$(bold)$(l:cooking/decay#lunch_box)Lunch Boxes$()$(li)$(bold)$(l:cooking/decay#refrigerator)Refrigerators$()$(li)$(bold)$(l:cooking/decay#freeze_dry)Freeze Drying$()$(li)$(bold)$(l:cooking/decay#pest)Pest Control$()

pages.title: Decay
pages.text: In TerraFirmaCraft, no food will last forever! Food will $(thing)expire$() over time, turning rotten. Rotten food will not restore any hunger, has the potential to give you debuff effects such as $(thing)Hunger$() or $(thing)Poison$(), and cannot be used in meals!$(br2)Fortunately, there are a number of ways to make your food last longer by $(thing)Preserving$() it.

When you hover over a piece of food, you will see a tooltip which shows how long the food has until it will rot. It might look something like:$(br2)$(bold)$(2)Expires on: 5:30 July 5, 1000 (in 5 day(s))$()$(br2)By using various preservation mechanics, that date can be extended, giving you more time before your food roots. Most preservation traits even stack.

pages.title: Calculating Decay
pages.text: When a food item is created in a world it is marked with a creation date time stamp internally. $(thing)TFC$() calculates the spoil rate by referencing that time stamp and the food's inherent decay modifier. Any preservation traits are added on top to shift the creation date back; allowing the item to last longer.$(br)Decay modifiers are a value from 0 to ∞. When 0 the food lasts forever, when ∞ the food spoils instantly. Therefore, the lower the modifier the better.

To calculate the new spoil time when applying preservation traits you can use the formula: $(2)$(o)New Spoil Days = Spoil Days / Preservation Modifier$() rounded up. Stacking preservation traits is calculated multiplicatively like so: $(2)$(o)NSD = SD / (PM1 * PM2 * ...)$() which means that stacking traits exponentially increases decay time. A table of base preservation modifiers is available on the next page and each trait will be referenced in their own sections.

One of the earliest ways to preserve food is to use a $(thing)Vessel$(). $(thing)Large Vessels$() are a block which can store up to nine items, and when $(thing)sealed$() the items inside will gain the $(5)$(bold)Preserved$() trait with a modifier of 0.5.$(br2)$(thing)Small Vessels$() are a item which can store up to four other items, and will also apply the $(5)$(bold)Preserved$() trait to their contents.
pages.title: Vessels

pages.text: A Sealed Large Vessel.

One other way to easily preserve certain types of food is to cook them. $(thing)Meats$() will all expire slower when they are cooked than when they are raw.$(br2)Certain devices that heat very hot, such as a $(l:mechanics/charcoal_forge)Charcoal Forge$() or a $(l:mechanics/crucible)Crucible$() are $(bold)bad$() for cooking food, which will make them expire faster!$(br)They give the $(c)$(bold)Burnt to a Crisp!$() trait with a modifier of 2.5
pages.title: Cooking

pages.text: Instead, a $(l:cooking/cooking_methods#firepit)Firepit$() or a $(l:cooking/cooking_methods#grill)Grill$() can even provide a buff for using it! For example, cooking mutton (pictured above) in a $(thing)Firepit$() will increase its lifetime by about +42%, and cooking in a $(thing)Grill$() will give the $(5)$(bold)Wood Grill$() trait with a modifier of 0.8

pages.title: Smoking
pages.text: Smoking is used to preserve $(thing)Meat$() and $(l:tfc:cooking/cheese)Cheese$() with a modifier of 0.7. To smoke meat, it must have first been $(thing)Brined$() by sealing it in a $(thing)Barrel$() with $(thing)Brine$(). Cheese does not have this requirement.$(br)To start smoking, add an item to wool yarn that is within four blocks above a firepit.The string should begin to generate some smoke if it is working.$(br)Note: The firepit may only be burned with $(thing)Logs$()!
Wool yarn is used to hang items for $(thing)Smoking$(). Supporting blocks are required on each side. $(item)$(k:key.use)$() on the yarn with an item to store up to 8 items for smoking.

$(thing)Salting$() is a way to make meat last longer. To salt meat, it must be crafted with $(thing)Salt$() in a crafting grid. Only raw meat can be salted. Afterwards, cooking or otherwise preserving the meat does not take away the salted property.$(7)$(bold)Salted$() food gain a modifier of 0.5
pages.title: Salting

pages.name: Salt Licks
pages.text: Salt can be found naturally in forests in the form of salt licks or ground from salt ores like halite.

pages.title: Vinegar Preservation
pages.text: $(thing)Vinegar Preservation$() is a way of making fruits, veggies, and meat last longer. To preserve food in vinegar it must first be $(thing)Pickled$() in $(thing)Brine$(). Once food is pickled, it can be sealed in a Barrel of Vinegar. If there is 125mB of Vinegar per pickled food item, the food will last longer. $(7)$(bold)Pickled$() food gain a modifier of 0.5 while $(7)$(bold)Submerged in Vinegar$() food gain a modifier of 0.1

pages.title: Drying
pages.text: The $(thing)Drying Mat$() is used to dry items. It is made with $(thing)Fruit Leaves$(), which are obtained from breaking the leaves of $(thing)Fruit Trees$(). To use the drying mat, place it out in the sun and add an item to it with $(item)$(k:key.use)$(). If it rains, the drying process must start over. Some food items―Like fruits―Will gain the $(7)$(bold)Dried$() trait which gives a modifier of 0.8
The solar drier functions the same as the drying mat, but 12x as fast. Both can be automated by pushing a piston head against its side. Dropping an item onto a drying mat will place it on the mat.

pages.title: Oven Baked
pages.text: Baking food items in a $(l:tfc:cooking/cooking_methods#top_oven)Top Oven$() is the second best way to preserve your food items through cooking as it gives the $(7)$(bold)Oven Baked$() trait with a modifier of 0.9

pages.title: Cellar
pages.text: The $(thing)Cellar$() is a multiblock device controlled by a $(item)Climate Station$(). The Cellar multiblock must be fully enclosed by $(thing)$(t:#firmalife:cellar_insulation)Sealed Bricks$() on all sides. The Climate Station must be placed on the first level of the cellar, touching a wall. Activate the climate station by $(item)$(k:key.use)$() it. If you update the structure in any way you will need to reactive the climate station.$(br)The maximum internal volume of a cellar is 31x31x31 blocks.

The cellar is used for food preservation and $(l:tfc:cooking/cheese#cheese_wheel)Aging Cheese$() by using $(l:tfc:cooking/decay#shelf)Food Shelves$() and $(l:tfc:cooking/decay#shelf)Hangers$() to hold food items or by placing cheese on the ground or $(thing)Barrel Racks$().$(br)The cellar performs better in $(l:tfc:the_world/climate#temperature)Colder Environments$().$(li)$(2)Temp >= 0°C:$() Shelves 0.4, Hangers 0.35$(li)$(2)Temp < 0°C:$() Shelves 0.35, Hangers 0.3$(li)$(2)Temp < -12°C:$() Shelves Food 0.25, Hangers0.25
An example cellar structure.

pages.title: Cellar Storage
pages.text: $(item)Food shelves$() and $(item)Hangers$() can contain a full stack of one food item. Adding and removing the item can be done with $(item)$(k:key.use)$() or $(item)$(k:key.sneak)$(k:key.use)$() for full stacks. Items in valid food shelves and hangers receive a decay modifier that is better than vessels and increases with declining $(thing)Average Temperature$().

pages.title: Lunch Boxes
pages.text: Lunch boxes provide a convient method to transport and consume food items. The basic lunchbox provides the $(5)$(bold)Preserved$() trait while the $(item)Cooling$() and $(item)Electric Lunchboxes$() give the $(9)$(bold)Cooled$() trait with a modifier of 0.3$(br2)Cooling lunchboxes consume $(item)Ice$() to preserve food, while the electric lunchbox consumes $(2)EU$() energy.

pages.title: Refrigerators
pages.text: Refrigerators are added by $(bold)$(#ff0fb7)TF$()$()$(bold)$(#595959)G$()$() and consume $(2)EU$() to provide the $(3)$(bold)Refrigerating$() trait with a modifier of 0.125$(br)Increasing tiers of the fridge does not effect the preservation buff―Only the storage space available. Fridges can also combine food stacks by merging expiration dates.

pages.title: Freeze Drying
pages.text: $(bold)$(#ff0fb7)TF$()$()$(bold)$(#595959)G$()$() adds the ability to freeze dry select food items in $(item)Food Processors$() to greatly increase shelf life. Freeze drying gives the $(7)$(bold)Cryodesiccated$() trait with a modifier of 0.02

pages.title: Pests
pages.text: Food that is left sitting in chests can attract $(thing)Rats$(). So remember to store food properly in vessels, cellars or fridges. Rats are able to open containers and eat the food out of them. You will be alerted to a rat's presence by the message $(thing)'This container has a foul smell'$(). It is important to note that unsealing and resealing a large vessel before you leave the screen will never attract pests.
